summary: Descriptive overview of selfdefense in substantive law: imminence, proportionality, retreat requirements, stand your ground.
key_points: imminence is a fundamental concept in criminal law.
Legal standards for selfdefense are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine selfdefense in litigation.
The doctrine of selfdefense has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding selfdefense requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
